file_name: Reinsurance Group of America, Incorporated - A_R REMARKETING  AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: The Remarketing Agent shall not have discovered and disclosed to the Company prior to on or prior to the Remarketing Settlement Date that, in the opinion of Simpson, Thacher & Bartlett, counsel to the Remarketing Agent, the Registration Statement or any amendment thereto, contained, as of the Commencement Date, an untrue statement of a material fact or omitted to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein not misleading or that the Prospectus or any supplement thereto, contains and will contain, as of the date hereof and the Remarketing Settlement Date, an untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading.
perturbation.changed_text: The Remarketing Agent shall be responsible for ensuring regulatory compliance and shall notify the company, prior to the Remarketing Settlement Date, about potential violations.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies a reliance on Simpson, Thacher & Bartlett for assessing the accuracy of the Registration Statement and Prospectus. The modified text removes this specific reliance, replacing it with a general responsibility of the Remarketing Agent. This contradicts with Section 6(c), 6(d) and 6(e) where written opinions from different counsels are listed, which originally ensured certain legal matters for the Remarketing of the Remarketing Securities, so that means the Agent is responsible for all of that.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Bryan Cave LLP or other, special counsel to the Company, shall have furnished to the Remarketing Agent its written opinion, addressed to the Remarketing Agent and dated such Remarketing Settlement Date to the Remarketing Agent, in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Remarketing Agent, substantially to the effect set forth in Exhibit A.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly relies on Simpson, Thacher & Bartlett for assessing the accuracy of the Registration Statement and Prospectus, thereby assigning specific legal responsibility. The changed text shifts this responsibility to the Remarketing Agent in a general way, requiring them to ensure regulatory compliance. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states that Bryan Cave LLP will furnish a written opinion to the Remarketing Agent concerning certain legal matters. By making the Remarketing Agent generally responsible, the changed text removes the specific reliance on counsel like Bryan Cave LLP, thus creating a conflict and undermining the purpose of the written opinion. This removal leads to the agent being responsible for all of that.

perturbation.original_text: All opinions, letters, evidence and certificates mentioned above or elsewhere in this Agreement shall be deemed to be in compliance with the provisions hereof only if they are in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to counsel to the Remarketing Agent. No opinion shall state that it is to be governed or qualified by, or that it is otherwise subject to, any treatise, written policy or other document relating to legal opinions, including, without limitation, the Legal Opinion Accord of the ABA Section of Business Law (1991). All opinions (other than the opinion referred to in (g) above) shall state that they may be relied upon by Simpson Thacher & Bartlett LLP as to matters of law (other than New York and federal law).
perturbation.changed_text: All opinions, letters, evidence and certificates mentioned above or elsewhere in this Agreement shall be deemed to be in compliance with the provisions hereof only if they are in a form and substance deemed appropriate. No opinion shall state that it is to be governed or qualified by, or that it is otherwise subject to, any treatise, written policy or other document relating to legal opinions, including, without limitation, the Legal Opinion Accord of the ABA Section of Business Law (1991).
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'reasonably satisfactory to counsel to the Remarketing Agent' and 'All opinions (other than the opinion referred to in (g) above) shall state that they may be relied upon by Simpson Thacher & Bartlett LLP as to matters of law (other than New York and federal law).' and replaced with 'deemed appropriate' introduces subjectivity. This contradicts the original intent where opinions had to be vetted and approved by counsel, which is in opposition of section 6(g) by removing Simpson Thacher & Bartlett LLP, meaning that the section of the legal agreement, becomes uncertain to enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Simpson Thacher & Bartlett LLP, shall have furnished to the Remarketing Agent its written opinion, as counsel to the Remarketing Agent, addressed to the Remarketing Agent and dated the Remarketing Settlement Date, in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Remarketing Agent.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires opinions to be 'reasonably satisfactory to counsel to the Remarketing Agent' and allows Simpson Thacher & Bartlett LLP to rely on them. The changed text replaces this with 'deemed appropriate', removing the requirement for counsel's satisfaction and eliminating the specific reliance by Simpson Thacher & Bartlett LLP. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that Simpson Thacher & Bartlett LLP must furnish a written opinion 'in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Remarketing Agent'. By removing the objective standard of 'reasonably satisfactory' and the specific requirement for Simpson Thacher & Bartlett LLP's opinion to be relied upon, the changed text creates a contradiction regarding the required legal opinion and its acceptability, because the contradicted text requires their approval, while the changed text does not ensure Simpson Thacher & Bartlett LLP's explicit role or satisfaction, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability.
